shlokas.title: Gananam Tva
shlokas.devanagari:
गणानां त्वा गणपतिं हवामहे
कविं कवीनामुपमश्रवस्तमम् ।
ज्येष्ठराजं ब्रह्मणां ब्रह्मणस्पत
आ नः शृण्वन्नूतिभिः सीद सादनम् ॥
श्री महा गणपतये नमः
shlokas.transliteration:
Gannaanaam Tvaa Ganna-Patim Havaamahe
Kavim Kaviinaam-Upama-Shravastamam |
Jyessttha-Raajam Brahmannaam Brahmannaspata
Aa Nah Shrnnvann-Uutibhih Siida Saadanam ||
Sri Mahaa Gannapataye Namaha
shlokas.english_meaning: O Lord of the Ganas, we invoke you, the Poet of poets, the most celebrated one, the chief of the knowledgeable, the lord of the sacred word — be present in this place of thought and action. Salutations to Lord Ganapati.

shlokas.title: Pranodevi Saraswati Mantra
shlokas.devanagari:
प्रणो देवी सरस्वती
वाजेभिर्वाजिनीवती
धीनामवित्र्यवतु
वादेव्यै नमः ॥
shlokas.transliteration:
Pranoo Devi Saraswati
Vaajebhir Vaajiniivati
Dhiinaam Avitryavatu
Vaagdevyai Namaha
shlokas.english_meaning: May the Divine Goddess Saraswati, source of strength and plenitude, protect our intellect, thoughts, and consciousness with her abundant grace. Salutations to the Goddess of speech.
shlokas.source_text: Rig Veda Mandala 1 · Sukta 3 · Mantras 10 to 12

shlokas.title: Lakshmi Gayatri Mantra
shlokas.devanagari:
ॐ महालक्ष्म्यै च विद्महे
विष्णुपत्नी च धीमहि
तन्नो लक्ष्मी प्रचोदयात् ॥
shlokas.transliteration:
Om Mahālakshmyai cha vidmahe
Vishnu-patnyai cha dhīmahi
Tanno Lakshmīi pracodayāt
shlokas.english_meaning: Om, we seek to know and understand the great Goddess Mahalakshmi. We meditate upon the consort of Vishnu. May that Lakshmi inspire and guide our intellect.

shlokas.title: Mahishasura Mardini
shlokas.devanagari:
अयि गिरिनन्दिनि नन्दितमेदिनि विश्वविनोदिनि नन्दिनुते
गिरिवरविन्ध्यशिरोऽधिनिवासिनि विष्णुविलासिनि जिष्णुनुते ।
भगवति हे शितिकण्ठकुटुम्बिनि भूरिकुटुम्बिनि भूरिकृते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १ ॥
shlokas.transliteration:
Ayi Giri-Nandini Nandita-Medini Vishva-Vinodini Nandi-Nute
Giri-Vara-Vindhya-Shiro-(A)dhi-Nivaasini Vishnnu-Vilaasini Jissnnu-Nute |
Bhagavati He Shiti-Kannttha Kuttumbini Bhuuri Kuttumbini Bhuuri Krte
Jaya Jaya He Mahissaasura-Mardini Ramya Kapardini Shaila-Sute || 1 ||
shlokas.english_meaning: Salutations to the Divine Mother. I invoke You, who is the Daughter of the Mountain, by whose presence the whole World is filled with Joy. For Whom the whole World is a Divine Play and who is praised by Nandi, who dwell on the summit of the Vindhyas, the Best of the Mountains, who give Joy to Lord Vishnu (as His sister) and who is praised by Lord Indra. O Goddess Bhagavati, who is the Consort of the One with Blue Throat (Lord Shiva), who has Many Many Relations in this World (being the Cosmic Mother) and who has created Abundance in Creation. Victory to You, Victory to You, who Slay the Demon Mahishasura, who shine with Beautiful Locks of Hair, and who is the Daughter of the Mountain.

shlokas.title: Maheshwari Gayatri Mantra
shlokas.devanagari:
वृषारुढायै विद्महे शूलहस्तायै धीमहि ।
तन्नो माहेश्वरी प्रचोदयात् ।
shlokas.transliteration:
Vrisharudhayai Vidmahe
Shulahastayai Dheemahi |
Tanno Maheswari Prachodayat
shlokas.english_meaning: We meditate on Her who rides the bull, holding the trident. May the great Maheshwari enlighten us.

shlokas.title: Kshama Prarthana
shlokas.devanagari:
यदक्षरपदभ्रष्टं मात्राहीनन्तु यद्भवेत् ।
तत्सर्वं क्षम्यतां देव नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
श्रीमते नारायणाय नमो नमः ॥
shlokas.transliteration:
Yad aksharapada bhrastam
maatraa hinam tu yad bhavet |
Tat sarvam kshamyataam deva
Naaraayana namostu te ||
Srimate Naaraayanaaya namo namah ||
shlokas.english_meaning: Whatever letters or words I have mispronounced or spoken with missing measures, may all that be forgiven, O Lord Narayana, I offer my salutations to You. Salutations again to the blessed Naaraayana.

shlokas.title: Mukundamala Stotra
shlokas.devanagari:
कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा
बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात् ।
करोमि यद्यत्सकलं परस्मै
नारायणायेति समर्पयामि ॥
सर्वं श्रीकृष्णार्पणमस्तु ॥
shlokas.transliteration:
Kāyena vāchā manasendriyair vā
Buddhyātmanā vā prakrtteh svabhāvāt |
Karomi yadyat sakalam parasmai
Nārāyanāyeti samarpayāmi ||
Sarvam Srī-Krishnaarpannam astu ||
shlokas.english_meaning: Whatever I do, with my body, speech, mind, senses, intellect, self, or by my natural disposition, I offer it all to the Supreme Narayana. May everything be an offering to Sri Krishna.
